meta_title: For Students: passive income to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Build a Portfolio Through Passive Income: Beginner Tutorial
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about no portfolio. Learn to build automated passive income streams using Antigravity and Claude Code in this step-by-step developer tutorial.
content.answer_block: Transform your 'no portfolio' status by building a micro-SaaS that generates passive income. By leveraging AI-assisted development tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can ship functional, revenue-generating tools in days. This approach proves your engineering capability and business acumen, making you an irresistible candidate to future employers.
content.intro:
The job market is a desert for those with nothing but theory. You sit at your desk, staring at a blank GitHub profile, feeling the crushing weight of the 'no portfolio' paradox: you need experience to get hired, but you need to be hired to get experience. It is a cycle that kills the ambition of most students before they even ship their first line of production code.

But here is the secret the gatekeepers won't tell you: the most impressive portfolio isn't a collection of tutorial clones—it is a live, revenue-generating product. By building a passive income tool, you demonstrate full-stack ownership, deployment mastery, and the ability to solve real-world problems. Today, we break the cycle. We are going to build, ship, and monetize.

steps.title: Define Your Micro-SaaS Niche
steps.description: Identify a small, repetitive task that people currently pay for. Focus on high-intent, low-complexity problems like automated report generation or data scraping.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior product architect. Suggest 5 low-code SaaS ideas that solve a specific pain point for small business owners and can be built in under 48 hours.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze current micro-SaaS trends for developers and identify a profitable niche for a student portfolio project.'

steps.title: Architect the Minimum Viable Product
steps.description: Keep the scope tight. You need authentication, a database, and a payment gateway. Don't over-engineer the UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a technical architecture diagram for a subscription-based tool using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Focus on simplicity and scalability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a Next.js project with Tailwind CSS and Supabase authentication boilerplate.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Focus on the 'value' logic. Whether it's an API wrapper or an AI-driven workflow, ensure the core engine works perfectly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust, production-ready function to handle Stripe webhooks and update user subscription status in Supabase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement the Stripe webhook handler and ensure database synchronization with user profiles.'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: A live link is worth more than 100 commits. Deploy your project to Vercel or Railway to make it accessible to real users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for production deployment, including environment variables, security headers, and database indexing strategies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure Vercel deployment settings and secure environment variables for production launch.'

steps.title: Launch and Iterate
steps.description: Push your tool to Product Hunt or niche forums. Your portfolio is now a live business that users interact with.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a compelling launch post for a developer-centric tool that highlights the problem-solving journey and the technical architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'How do I monitor user feedback and error logs in production to improve my SaaS after launch?'

pro_tips: Always focus on the 'User Journey' over the 'Code Complexity' when presenting your project in interviews.
Use a single database schema for your first 3 projects to build a reusable 'boilerplate' library.
Document your deployment failures in a blog post; recruiters value post-mortem analysis more than perfect launches.
Implement automated error tracking (like Sentry) early; it shows you care about production stability.
Treat your SaaS as a product, not a project—add a 'Changelog' page to show you understand iterative development.
content.next_steps: Once your app is live, document the architecture and the revenue metrics in your portfolio. Reach out to three potential users for feedback, iterate on the product, and prepare to present your 'live business' in your next technical interview.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder with a live product. Stop waiting for permission to be an engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
